data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Spot Prices Rise as International Prices Drop, EIA Reports
data.description:
U.S. natural gas spot prices rose at most locations in the week ended March 5, with the benchmark Henry Hub spot price gaining US$0.49 to US$4.40/million British thermal units, the U.S. Energy Information Administration said in its Natural Gas Weekly Update released Thursday.

Liquefied natural gas prices in East Asia lost US$0.15 to US$13.87/MMBtu, the EIA said, citing data from Bloomberg Finance. Title Transfer Facility natural gas prices in Europe fell US$0.43 to US$13.55/MMBtu.

U.S. natural gas supply edged 0.7% higher as higher dry natural gas production offset a 21% drop in average net imports from Canada, the EIA said, citing data from S&P Global Commodity Insights.

U.S. natural gas consumption fell 15%, as residential and commercial sector use declined by 21% amid warmer weather. Natural gas consumed for power also declined 14% and industrial sector use fell 3.9%, the agency said.

Net withdrawals from storage amounted to 80 billion cubic feet for the week ended Feb. 28, resulting in total working gas stocks of 1,760 Bcf, the EIA reported. This was 224 Bcf lower than the five-year average and 585 Bcf lower than the year-ago period.

data.title: Berenberg cuts Neste to 'hold' on margin pressure
data.description:
** Berenberg downgrades Finnish oil refiner and biofuel maker Neste to "hold" from "buy", as margin pressure is set to persist

** The company's new strategy reflects the much tougher operating environment, it says, seeing margins to remain under pressure given worsening industry overcapacity

** However, it sees significant upside potential in the longer term should margins recover

** The broker sees supply capacity set to nearly double to 37mtpa by 2028, and even assuming strong demand growth, it is likely that the market will be oversupplied until at least 2028

** The brokerage says the continued uncertainty over the Clean Fuel Production Credit (CFPC) in the U.S. could be another significant headwind for earnings

** It cuts target price by 47% to 10 euros

** Out of 24 analysts that cover the Neste stock, nine rate it "strong buy" or "buy", 13 rate it "hold", and two rate "sell"

data.title: India Shares Close Flat as Bank Stocks Lead Declines — Market Talk
data.description: Indian shares ended flat as investors watched for further developments on tensions surrounding U.S. trade policies. Among the top gainers, Reliance Industries rose 3.2%, Nestle India was up 1.6% and Tata Motors gained 1.4%. Meanwhile, bank stocks led the losses, with IndusInd Bank down 3.5% and Yes Bank lower by 0.6%. NTPC and Infosys were also down, by 2.5% and 1.6%, respectively. The benchmark Sensex closed flat at 74332.58. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Berenberg Adjusts Price Target on Pfizer to $28 From $29, Maintains Hold Rating
data.description: Pfizer has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $30.56, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Stifel Adjusts Price Target on Microsoft to $475 From $515, Maintains Buy Rating
data.description: Microsoft has an average rating of buy and mean price target of $508.26, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Ming Fai International's Profit to Rise in 2024
data.description:
Ming Fai International expects an attributable profit of at least HK$140 million for the year 2024, up from HK$104.2 million in the year-ago period, a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

The hygiene product provider attributed the anticipated higher profit mainly to increased revenue from its businesses of hospitality supplies and operating supplies and equipment, as well as to an improved gross profit margin.

data.title: Carl Zeiss Extends Lease at Life Science REIT Facility
data.description:
Life Science REIT tenant Carl Zeiss signed a lease extension at Cambourne Park Science & Technology Campus in Cambourne, UK.

The tenant, occupying 43,300 square feet, extended the lease to 2033 from 2028, the real estate company said Friday.

(i)         Writing, selling, purchasing or varying

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description e.g. call option

  • Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.

  • Number of securities to which option relates

  • Exercise price per unit

  • Type

    e.g. American, European etc.

  • Expiry date

  • Option money paid/ received per unit

(ii)        Exercise

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description

    e.g. call option

  • Exercising/ exercised against

  • Number of securities

  • Exercise price per unit

(d)        Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)

  • Class of relevant security

  • Nature of dealing

    e.g. subscription, conversion

  • Details

  • Price per unit (if applicable)

4.         OTHER INFORMATION

(a)        Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the person making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

    Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included.  If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the person making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

    (i)  the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

    (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:

    If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None

(c)        Attachments

  • Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?

  • NO

Date of disclosure:

07/03/2025

Contact name:

Paul Jenkins

Telephone number:

+44 20 7676 6969

Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service.

The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.

The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk.

Total AssetsNet asset value per Ordinary share
including current year income and expensesincluding current year income and expenses
  £170.592 million  1466.44p
   The Company does not generate sufficient current year revenue receipts in excess of current year  revenue expenses for the net asset value per Ordinary Share including current year income and   expenses to exceed the net asset value per Ordinary share excluding current year income and   expenses (the 'capital net asset value'). Accordingly, the above figures may also be used as a   conservative estimate of the capital net asset value in each case.     The latest monthly fact sheet and copies of the company's most recent interim and full annual reports   accounts are available for download from  https://www.brownadvisory.com/intl/investment-trusts/brown-advisory-us-smaller-companies-plc.        Enquiries:  FundRock Partners Limited, Company Secretary  Limor Gonen  Tel:+44 203 994 7129  ukfundscosec@apexfs.com  This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange.
